thought_beat_text: The overhead light catches the edge of the flat and he sees exactly where the world stops being world — foam, paint, a staple — and the reversal he's been imagining, the one where he simply says it aloud, keeps collapsing because saying it would mean admitting he's been standing inside a lie and calling it ground. The darkness past the edge isn't rest; it's the proof that the ring, the checkpoint, all of it was always a stage, and he doesn't know yet if that makes his sincerity inside it false or the only real thing left.
residue_summary: He has seen the seam clearly now and cannot rebuild the illusion, only decide whether what he felt inside it still counts for anything.
image_hint: a staple gleaming at the edge of painted foam under a hard overhead light
audio_hint: low fluorescent hum with a single unresolved chord beneath
